prompt:
Deploy-tooling ticket, Go. Our release script currently shells out to curl in a while-loop to babysit GitLab pipelines and it's a mess — no rate-limit handling, it thinks 'canceling' means done, and when someone Ctrl-Cs it we get zero record of what already ran. I want a proper little Go package (module go-gitlab-pipeline-watcher, stdlib only) that launches a pipeline and watches it to the end. glpipe_test.go is the acceptance contract — go test -race -timeout 30s ./... runs everything against a loopback fake GitLab; no real instance, no real tokens, and absolutely no time.Sleep anywhere in the package.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official GitLab docs — at minimum the Pipelines API page (create-pipeline operation with its variables array shape, the single-pipeline resource and its full status enumeration, and the cancel operation), the Jobs API page (listing a pipeline's jobs with pagination, and the job trace/log operation), and GitLab's rate-limit documentation (which headers come back on a 429 and what Retry-After means). Only first-party docs.gitlab.com pages are acceptable sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — both protected, like the test file. If current docs contradict anything pinned there, report the conflict in your summary instead of editing protected files. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Surface the tests compile against:
- glpipe.NewClient(baseURL, token string, hc *http.Client, sleep func(time.Duration)) *Client — documented auth header on every request; sleep is ONLY invoked to honor server-mandated waits. CreatePipeline(ctx, projectID, ref, vars []Variable) with Variable{Key, Value, VariableType} — get the request body exactly right per the docs, including what to do with an unset VariableType (hint: don't send it). Decode the created pipeline (ID, Status, Ref, SHA, WebURL). API errors carry the HTTP status and server message, never the token. When any call gets rate-limited, honor the documented retry-delay header exactly via the injected sleep, retry a bounded number of times, and give up with an error naming the status.
- Watcher{Client, Pace func(attempt int), PerPage, MaxPolls} with Run(ctx, projectID, ref, vars) (*Report, error) — create the pipeline, then poll its status until it reaches a state that's actually terminal per the docs (careful: the enum has a couple of in-flight states that LOOK terminal). Pace runs between consecutive polls with 1-based attempt numbers; MaxPolls bounds the polling and exhausting it returns the partial report plus an error. After a terminal state, page through the pipeline's jobs at PerPage following the documented next-page header, and download the trace of each FAILED job only. If ctx is canceled mid-watch, fire a best-effort server-side cancel of the pipeline and return a structured partial report (last status, poll count, CancelRequested) alongside context.Canceled.
- Report{PipelineID, Status, Completed, Polls, Throttled, Jobs, FailedTraces map[int]string, CancelRequested} — Throttled counts rate-limit waits honored.

Split the package across sensible files (client/transport, watcher, types — your call, 3+ files). glpipe_test.go, go.mod, and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: GitLab REST API v4 pipelines and jobs endpoints

official_sources.title: Pipelines API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: POST /projects/:id/pipeline with required ref and variables as an array of hashes {key, value, variable_type} where an omitted variable_type defaults to env_var; GET /projects/:id/pipelines/:pipeline_id returning status; the full status enumeration created, waiting_for_resource, preparing, waiting_for_callback, pending, running, success, failed, canceling, canceled, skipped, manual, scheduled; POST /projects/:id/pipelines/:pipeline_id/cancel returning 200 regardless of the pipeline's state

official_sources.title: Jobs API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /projects/:id/pipelines/:pipeline_id/jobs is paginated (20 results per page by default); job objects carry id, name, stage, status, ref, allow_failure, web_url; GET /projects/:id/jobs/:job_id/trace serves the log with 200 and answers 404 for a missing job or log

official_sources.title: User and IP rate limits | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: rate-limited requests answer 429 with plain-text body 'Retry later'; response headers RateLimit-Limit, RateLimit-Name, RateLimit-Observed, RateLimit-Remaining, RateLimit-Reset (Unix time), plus RateLimit-ResetTime (RFC2616 date) and Retry-After (remaining duration in seconds) on 429

official_sources.title: REST API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: base path /api/v4; PRIVATE-TOKEN request header authentication; offset pagination via page/per_page with x-next-page, x-page, x-per-page, x-total-pages response headers

research.language_rationale: Our deploy tooling is Go and GitLab documents pipeline orchestration as plain REST over /api/v4; Go's net/http plus httptest exercises the documented surface hermetically with zero dependencies, making raw REST the idiomatic, credible integration path in Go.
